password.title: Login Password
password.desc: Set a new login password for this account. It takes effect immediately after saving.

email.title: Security Email
email.desc: Used for notifications and email-code login. You can replace or clear it without verification.
email.placeholder: Enter security email; leave empty and save to clear
email.clearConfirm: Clearing the security email will also disable email MFA. Continue?

emailMfa.title: Email MFA
emailMfa.desc: When enabled, login requires an email code. It cannot be enabled without a security email or while TOTP is on.

totp.title: TOTP MFA
totp.desc: An admin can only turn off this account's TOTP; it cannot be enabled on the user's behalf.
totp.disableConfirm: Disable TOTP MFA for this account?

loginMethods.title: Login Methods
loginMethods.desc: Enable or disable login methods for this account. Disabled methods cannot be enabled or used by the user, and at least one must remain.

thirdParty.desc: Manage this account's third-party login channels: enable or disable a channel. Disabled channels cannot be enabled or used by the user.

messages.passwordLength: Password length must be between 6 and 32 characters

placeholders.passwordOptional: Leave blank to keep the current password

messages.cannotDeleteSelf: The current logged-in user cannot delete themselves
messages.cannotDeleteAccount: You do not have permission to delete this account

selfRestricted.title: Action not allowed on current account
selfRestricted.deleteContent: You cannot delete the currently logged-in account from account management. You can cancel your own account via Account Center - Account Settings.
selfRestricted.cancelContent: You cannot cancel the currently logged-in account from account management. You can cancel your own account via Account Center - Account Settings.

validation.roleNameLength: Length must be between 2 and 20 characters
validation.roleCodeLength: Length must be between 2 and 50 characters

messages.missingRoleId: The current role data is missing roleId. Please verify the list API response.

messages.batchDeleteBound: {roles} are in use. Deleting will remove the association between the roles and their accounts. Are you sure you want to delete the selected {count} item(s)?

placeholders.name: Please enter menu name
placeholders.label: For example: User
placeholders.roles: Enter role mark and press Enter, for example: R_SUPER

tooltips.path:
Top-level menu: absolute path starting with / (for example /dashboard)
Second level and below: relative path (for example console or user)
tooltips.component:
Top-level parent menu: use /index/index
Specific page: use the component path (for example /system/account)
Directory menu: leave blank
tooltips.icon: Select a supported project icon. The ri: icon code will be filled automatically.
tooltips.roles:
Used only in frontend permission mode: configure role marks (for example R_SUPER, R_ADMIN)
Backend permission mode does not require this.
tooltips.activePath:
Used for hidden detail pages to highlight the parent menu path.
For example, the user detail page highlights the User Management menu.

validation.nameLength: Length must be between 2 and 20 characters

loginMethods.desc: Choose allowed login methods; keep at least one. Methods outside the global policy are disabled.
loginMethods.adminDisabled: Disabled by an administrator, temporarily unavailable
loginMethods.atLeastOne: At least one login method must remain

mfa.title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
mfa.desc: Require an additional verification step when signing in. TOTP and Email MFA are mutually exclusive; TOTP is recommended.
mfa.mutualExclusive: TOTP and Email MFA cannot be enabled at the same time

email.tip: Once enabled, you must enter a 6-digit code sent to your email when signing in
email.hint: Once enabled, sign-ins and sensitive actions require an extra email code. Make sure this email can receive messages normally.
email.enableTip: Once enabled, you must enter a 6-digit code sent to your email when signing in.
email.disableTip: Before disabling Email MFA, verify this action with an email code.

totp.desc: Use an authenticator app such as Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator or 1Password to generate one-time codes
totp.tip: Scan the QR code below with an RFC 6238 compatible authenticator app, or enter the secret manually, then enter the generated code to finish binding.
totp.scanTip: Scan the QR code above with your authenticator app
totp.secretLabel: Secret (use when scanning is not possible)
totp.codePlaceholder: Enter the {digits}-digit code from your app
totp.recoveryCodesTip: These recovery codes are shown only once. If your authenticator is unavailable, choose “Recovery code” in “Disable TOTP” to use one. Each recovery code can be used only once, so store them safely now.
totp.codeHint: Use the current code shown in your authenticator app to disable TOTP.
totp.recoveryCodeHint: Enter one of the recovery codes you saved after enabling TOTP. Each code can be used only once.
totp.recoveryCodePlaceholder: Enter a recovery code like ABCD-1234
totp.recoveryCodeFormat: Please enter a recovery code like ABCD-1234
totp.disableTip: You can disable TOTP with the current authenticator code or a recovery code you saved earlier.

account.title: Account Settings
account.desc: Manage your sign-in account name and account deletion settings.

username.title: Sign-in account
username.desc: Update the account name used for sign-in. Uniqueness is checked before saving.
username.label: Account name

validation.length: Account name must be between 3 and 12 characters

deletion.title: Delete account
deletion.desc: Once your account is deleted, it cannot be recovered. Please proceed carefully.
deletion.impactPrimary: Deleting your account immediately signs out all active sessions for this account.
deletion.impactSecondary: The account then enters a 14-day cooling-off period. You can recover it from the login page during that window.
deletion.impactHint: Before continuing, complete the required identity verification and enter the exact account name to confirm.
deletion.withEmailDesc: A security email is bound to {email}. Verify the email code first, then enter the account name to confirm deletion.
deletion.withPasswordDesc: No security email is bound. Verify your current password first, then enter the account name to confirm deletion.
deletion.usernameConfirm: Please enter your username "{username}" to confirm account deletion
deletion.codeSent: Verification code sent. Please check your inbox.
deletion.mfaBlocking: Disable all enabled MFA methods in Account Security before deleting the account.

mfaBlockingDialog.title: Account deletion unavailable
mfaBlockingDialog.content:
MFA is currently enabled for this account.
Go to "Account Center - Account Security - MFA" and disable the verification step before deleting the account.

deletion.pendingTip: This account is already in the deletion cooling-off period.
deletion.pendingStatus: Account is pending deletion and expires at {time}.

steps.confirm: Step 2/2: Enter the account name to confirm

validation.usernameMismatch: The account name does not match

thirdParty.desc: Bind a third-party account to sign in with it

messages.onlyImageAllowed: Only image files can be uploaded!

messages.invalidSizeValue: Please enter a valid file size.
messages.invalidSizeRange: Invalid file size range. The minimum value cannot exceed the maximum value.
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete file "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

placeholders.name: Please enter config name
placeholders.objectPrefix: Optional, for example corp-a or app/uploads
placeholders.sessionToken: Optional, fill in when using STS temporary credentials
placeholders.s3Domain: Use the default domain automatically when empty

tooltips.storage: Select the file storage type. If some storage capabilities are unavailable in the current environment or required runtime dependencies are missing, they will be hidden.
tooltips.dbDomain: Optional. Used to compose the access URL for database storage. When empty, the system download API is typically used.
tooltips.localBasePath: Files will be written to the server's local disk directory. Make sure the service process has read and write permissions.
tooltips.localDomain: Used to compose the file access URL. When empty, a relative path or the system default address is typically returned.
tooltips.ftpMode: FTP transfer mode, Active or Passive.
tooltips.ftpBasePath: FTP remote root directory. Files will continue to be stored in subdirectories under this path.
tooltips.remoteDomain: Optional. Used to compose the file access URL. When empty, a relative path /api/file/{configId}/get/{path} is returned.
tooltips.sftpBasePath: SFTP remote root directory. Files will continue to be stored in subdirectories under this path.
tooltips.endpoint: Object storage service endpoint, such as the access domain provided by OSS, MinIO, or COS.
tooltips.bucket: Bucket name. Objects will be uploaded to this bucket.
tooltips.accessKey: Access key ID. Used together with Access Secret.
tooltips.accessSecret: Access key secret. Used together with Access Key.
tooltips.objectPrefix: Optional. Used as a common prefix for all object keys, for example corp-a/uploads.
tooltips.sessionToken: Optional. Fill in only when using STS temporary credentials. Leave empty for long-term AK/SK scenarios.
tooltips.s3Domain: Optional. Used to override the default access address, such as a CDN domain or custom domain.
tooltips.region: Optional. The region where the object storage is located, such as cn-beijing or ap-guangzhou. When empty, the backend will try to infer it from the endpoint.
tooltips.enablePathStyleAccess: When enabled, uses endpoint/bucket/key. When disabled, typically uses bucket.endpoint/key. This is often required for MinIO and similar services.
tooltips.enablePublicAccess: When enabled, returns a public access URL. When disabled, returns a signed access URL.
tooltips.configJson: A structured form is not provided for the current storage type. Please enter the full JSON config directly.

messages.missingConfigId: The current config is missing id. Please verify the list API response.
messages.completeAccessKeyAndSecret: Please complete Access Key and Access Secret
messages.unsupportedStorageTitle: Some storage capabilities are unavailable in the current environment
messages.unsupportedStorageDescription: The following storage types are unavailable in the current environment or missing required runtime dependencies. You can still fill in the configuration, but the actual service will be unavailable: {storages}
messages.confirmDisableMaster: This config is the master config. Disabling it may cause file upload failures or data loss. Continue?
messages.confirmSetDisabledMaster: This config is disabled and may cause upload failures or data loss. Continue switching?
messages.confirmSetMaster: Are you sure you want to set "{name}" as the master config?
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete config "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

placeholders.name: Please enter a mailbox name
placeholders.username: Login account, usually the email address
placeholders.passwordCreate: Please enter the password or auth code
placeholders.passwordEdit: Leave blank to keep the current password / auth code
placeholders.fromAddress: Optional, defaults to the login account
placeholders.fromName: Optional, the sender name recipients see
placeholders.host: Leave blank to auto-detect by email domain

tooltips.group: A mailbox belongs to a single group. Groups are a filter of the sending pool; disabling a group removes its mailboxes from the pool.
tooltips.channel: The channel is the sending protocol (e.g. SMTP / SMTPS) and is also a filter of the sending pool.
tooltips.host: SMTP server host. Leave blank to auto-detect common provider settings by email domain.
tooltips.port: SMTP port. Leave blank to auto-select based on SSL (e.g. 465 / 587).
tooltips.ssl: Whether to use an SSL-encrypted connection. Leave blank for auto; usually on for SMTPS.
tooltips.starttls: Whether to upgrade the connection with STARTTLS. Leave blank for auto.
tooltips.weight: The lower the number, the higher the priority and the greater the chance of being selected. A minimum-usage guarantee ensures lower-weight mailboxes are never used more than higher-weight ones.
tooltips.failThreshold: After this many consecutive send failures, the mailbox is marked abnormal.
tooltips.retryInterval: After being marked abnormal, the mailbox is retried again once this many seconds elapse.
tooltips.maxUnavailable: Once the mailbox has been marked abnormal this many times in total, it is marked dead and can only be recovered manually.

messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete group "{name}"?

channel.title: Sending Channels
channel.tip: Check the channels that should take effect. Only mailboxes within active channels participate in the sending pool.

messages.missingId: This mailbox is missing an id; please check the list response
messages.enterPassword: Please enter the password or auth code
messages.testPromptPlaceholder: Enter the destination mailbox to receive the test email
messages.testEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address
messages.testSuccess: Test email sent. Please check the destination mailbox to verify availability.
messages.confirmRecover: Are you sure you want to recover mailbox "{name}"? This resets failure counters and resumes sending.
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete mailbox "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

placeholders.name: Please enter a template name
placeholders.subject: Enter the email subject; variables are allowed
placeholders.subjectZh: Enter the Chinese email subject; variables are allowed
placeholders.subjectEn: Enter the English email subject; variables are allowed
placeholders.content: Enter the email body HTML source; you can use the variables above

tooltips.type: The sending type maps to a specific sending scenario. Templates are rendered by sending type and recipient language. Sending type cannot be changed once created.
tooltips.sendType: The sending type maps to a specific sending scenario. Templates are rendered by sending type and recipient language. Sending type cannot be changed once created.
tooltips.enabled: At most one template is enabled per sending type; enabling this one disables the others in the same group.
tooltips.content: The body is HTML source using variable placeholders; the backend substitutes real values and HTML-escapes them when sending.

preview.title: Email Preview
preview.sampleTip: Below is a preview rendered with sample data; variables are replaced with example values and nothing is actually sent.

editor.noBlockSelected: Select a component on the canvas to edit its properties
editor.emptyState: Drag or click a component from the left panel to start building your email
editor.sourceReadonly: Source is read-only and generated automatically from the visual editor.

properties.contentPlaceholder: Enter text content; you can use {{variable}} placeholders

messages.missingId: This template is missing an id; please check the list response
messages.previewEmpty: The body is empty; please fill in the email body first
messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete template "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

accessControl.title: Access Control
accessControl.description: Centrally manage global switches for pages accessible before login. Disabled features hide their entry on the corresponding page and are rejected by the backend.
accessControl.loadFailed: Failed to load access control config

generalRegister.title: General Registration
generalRegister.description: When disabled, the login page hides the registration entry and the register API is rejected.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot Password
forgotPassword.description: When disabled, the login page hides the "Forgot password" entry and the recovery API is rejected.

loginMethod.title: Login Methods
loginMethod.description: Controls available login methods. Even if password is disabled, the login page still shows the full form, but submission is rejected by the backend.
loginMethod.atLeastOne: Keep at least one login method

thirdPartyLogin.title: Third-party Login
thirdPartyLogin.description: Controls available third-party login channels. Disabled channels hide their entry on the login page.

thirdPartyRegister.title: Third-party Registration
thirdPartyRegister.description: Controls available third-party registration channels. Third-party registration reuses the third-party login entry; disabling only affects registration.
